stem: A automotive testing study measures the fuel consumption (L/100km) for a sample of 20 vehicles. The sample mean is 424.1 L/100km with a sample standard deviation of 47.8 L/100km. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 424.1 L/100km. 95% CI = [401.7, 446.5]. Point estimate: 424.1 L/100km.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
